output.title: 10 Signs Your Landlord Is Illegally Withholding Your Security Deposit (And How to Fight Back)
output.meta_description: Landlord keeping your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights and demand your money back in 60 seconds for just $19.99—no signup needed.
content_outline: The Silent Treatment: When 'Processing' Becomes Theft
Normal Wear and Tear vs. Damage: What They Can Actually Deduct
State-by-State Deadline Cheat Sheet: How Long Is Too Long?
Can My Landlord Really Keep My Deposit? Know Your Rights
Why a Demand Letter Works Better Than a Text Rant
How to Send a Legally Formatted Dispute Letter in 60 Seconds
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 30 days with an itemized deduction list
Landlords cannot deduct for normal wear and tear, hypothetical damages, or emotional distress
A formal demand letter triggers statutory timelines and often forces immediate compliance without court
Deposits-Back.com generates state-specific demand letters for $19.99 in 60 seconds with no signup or subscription
output.cta: Stop waiting. Get your security deposit back in 60 seconds for just $19.99 at Deposits-Back.com. No signup. No subscription. Just results.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are withholding, delaying, ghosting, or wrongfully deducting security deposits
social_hooks: POV: Your landlord kept your $2,000 deposit for 'cleaning fees' but you have move-out photos that prove otherwise.
If your landlord hasn't returned your deposit in 30 days, they might owe you DOUBLE. Here's the law they don't want you to know.
My landlord said 'normal wear and tear' means he keeps the deposit. Sir, that's not how this works.
Stop sending angry texts to your landlord. A formal demand letter hits different—and it takes 60 seconds.
Landlord ghosting you after move-out? That's not 'processing.' That's theft. Here's how to respond.
Can my landlord keep my deposit if I break my lease? Only for unpaid rent. Not for revenge. Know your rights.
